instruction:
Add comprehensive edge case tests for `to_words`.


Existing tests for reference:
```r

```


The existing tests cover basic functionality. Add tests for:
1. NULL inputs to each argument
2. Empty vectors/strings: character(0), "", integer(0)
3. Boundary values: 0, 1, -1, NA, NaN, Inf
4. Invalid input types (passing numeric where character expected, etc.)
5. Missing arguments (use expect_error with class matching)
6. Very long inputs (strings > 1000 chars, vectors > 10000 elements)
context:
Package: stringr v1.6.0.9000
Description: Simple, Consistent Wrappers for Common String Operations
Function: to_words
Parameters: string
Test patterns used: test_that
Expectations used: expect_equal

Function Code:
```r
to_words <- function(string) {
  breakpoints <- paste(
    # non-word characters
    "[^\\p{L}\\p{N}]+",
    # lowercase followed by uppercase
    "(?<=\\p{Ll})(?=\\p{Lu})",
    # letter followed by number
    "(?<=\\p{L})(?=\\p{N})",
    # number followed by letter
    "(?<=\\p{N})(?=\\p{L})",
    # uppercase followed uppercase then lowercase (i.e. end of acronym)
    "(?<=\\p{Lu})(?=\\p{Lu}\\p{Ll})",
    sep = "|"
  )
  out <- str_replace_all(string, breakpoints, " ")
  out <- str_to_lower(out)
  str_trim(out)
}
```
